title: Hibiscus ovalifolius
intro: Hibiscus ovalifolius, also known as the oval-leaf hibiscus, is a plant species belonging to the mallow family (Malvaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including lowering blood pressure, reducing inflammation, and acting as a natural diuretic. The bioactive constituents of H. ovalifolius include flavonoids, anthocyanins, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with H. ovalifolius include tea infusions, capsules, and dried extracts, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of hypertension and cardiovascular diseases.
taxonomy: Hibiscus ovalifolius is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It is native to Australia and is part of the genus Hibiscus, which includes over 200 species of hibiscus plants.
